item.title: WWE 2K26 Locker Codes: All Active Codes For March 2026
item.content:encoded:
WWE 2K26 locker codes are one of the easiest ways to unlock rewards for MyFACTION throughout the game’s lifecycle. These free, one-time use codes can often be found across social media, but only if you know where to look. Thankfully, Insider Gaming keeps a lookout for you to make getting the best locker codes in WWE 2K26 as easy as bookmarking and visiting this page.

Below you’ll find all active WWE2K26 locker codes, what they unlock, and whether there is an expiration date. Codes are updated frequently, so expired ones are removed to keep this list current. If you find any issues or have any more codes to suggest that we may have missed, leave a comment below.

FIRST PUBLISHED: March 18, 2026
LAST UPDATED: March 18, 2026. The codes below are active as of March 2026.

What Are WWE 2K26 Locker Codes?

Locker codes are pretty straightforward. They’re free one-time use codes that unlock benefits in the game, and can either be permanent or available for a limited time.

When entering the codes, you don’t have to worry about capitalization as they aren’t case sensitive. However, make sure you include the hyphens for them to be viewed as legitimate. These codes can also be redeemed on every platform, unless otherwise noted.

How To Redeem WWE 2K26 Locker Codes?

To redeem your WWE 2K26 locker codes within the game, it’s quite simple:

  1. Scroll right on the main menu and select Locker Codes
  2. Enter any of the codes listed below and press start
  3. Go to Unopened Packs to open the packs and enjoy your rewards

If you don’t happen to be near your console or PC but want to redeem a code, you are going to find yourself out of luck. Unlike NBA 2K26, WWE 2K26 doesn’t have a companion app that allows locker code redemption while on the go.

How Much Do WWE 2K26 Codes Cost?

Never pay for WWE 2K26 locker codes. These codes come directly from the developer and other sources, and are completely free for all players. Anyone trying to sell you WWE 2K26 codes is doing so to scam you and should be reported. Again, WWE 2K26 locker codes are free. If you want to spend actual money, you can buy VC within the game itself.

When Do Locker Codes Expire?

WWE 2K26 locker codes usually expire a week after being released. That said, some codes will never expire. For codes that have no expiration, they will be marked. As codes expire, they will be removed from the active list to make sure only working ones appear.

  • MORE WWE 2K26: WWE 2K26 Soundtrack Revealed — Linkin Park, Bad Omens, More Featured

Are There One-Time Only Codes?

While all codes can only be used by each user once, some WWE 2K26 locker codes will be awarded via Twitch Drops or a different type of giveaway. Those are unique to the player who earns them, making it pointless to share in this article.

Active WWE 2K26 Locker Codes

As of publishing, there are no active locker codes for WWE 2K26. As the days, weeks, and months go by, there will be more to redeem. Make sure to bookmark this page and check back often to make sure you get every reward possible.

Here are the currently active WWE 2K26 codes and the rewards they provide:

(*) Codes marked with an asterisk in the reward column do not expire.

Expired WWE 2K26 Codes

Over time, codes will expire. Below you can find a list of past 2K26 codes that no longer work. Why keep them up? To show you what you could have had, obviously.

  • BSTINTWRLD26 – 1 Aftershock Series Basic Pack, 1 Momentum Series Basic Back
  • MZFGCPCR – 32,500 VC

How To Get More Locker Codes?

Locker codes are usually announced via the game’s official social media channels. However, sometimes they can come directly from teams or other means. If you want to make sure you always get the most recent active codes, your best option is to bookmark this page and check back regularly.

WWE 2K26 FAQs

What are WWE 2K26 locker codes?

Locker codes are free codes from 2K that unlock rewards in MyFACTION.

Do WWE 2K26 locker codes expire?

Most codes are limited-time offerings, usually around a week or so. Some never expire and are clearly marked in our list of active codes.

How can I redeem WWE 2K26 locker codes?

You can claim codes in-game by navigating to Locker Codes in the Main Menu and entering your codes when prompted.

Are WWE 2K26 locker codes free?

Yes, all WWE 2K26 locker codes are free and distributed by 2K. Be cautious of anyone attempting to sell them.

How do I get new WWE 2K26 codes?

New codes are shared via official WWE 2K social media, promotions, or events. Bookmark this page for the latest updates.

What types of rewards can I get from locker codes?

Rewards include MyFACTION packs, VC, MFP, Persona Cards, and more.

Can I share WWE 2K26 locker codes?

The locker codes can only be redeemed one time per account, so you can share the codes with your friends as long as they have a separate account.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out every trophy and achievement for WWE 2K26 and how to unlock them. And don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter and join the Insider Gaming Discord.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Gray Zone Warfare 0.4 Update Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
MADFINGER Games has revealed that Gray Zone Warfare’s 0.4 update will be released globally on March 31. This is the super-sized upgrade that introduces stacks of new content to the game and implements an overhaul that feels enough to almost call it Gray Zone Warfare 2.0.

The update, which will be free for all users, will land with Support Edition owners from March 27th, rewarding them for their investment in this open-world tactical shooter.

Gray Zone Warfare 0.4 Update is Nuts

The sheer scope and scale of Gray Zone Warfare’s 0.4 update is dizzying. From what we’ve seen in developer posts and videos, the upgrade will introduce new tasks, locations, weapons, ways to play, and ways to work with other users.

In a string of enhancements, the game’s textures, lighting, ambient sounds, movement mechanics, and combat features have all been boosted, and everything from the map to the game’s onboarding features has been overhauled. It’s quite simply a mind-blowing update that’s bursting at the seams with content and new assets.

If you’ve been waiting for a reason to jump into Gray Zone Warfare (or back into…), then this might just be it.

The final developer video went live recently, talking through the upgraded task system, the new tactical map, the reputation levels with vendors and progression paths, the new lore and backstory aspects, and the enhanced loot pool and 500 new items coming to the game with 0.4.

It has been a long time coming, of course. The 0.3 update for Gray Zone Warfare was released almost a year ago, introducing ‘Winds of War’. That dropped after the Night Ops update that was added in November 2024.

It’s safe to say that, from the scale alone, the 0.4 update, named ‘Spearhead’, blows them out of the water.

Are you going to be enjoying Spearhead when it’s released in Gray Zone Warfare on March 31? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Marathon’s ranked play goes live soon

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Marathon Ranked Play Begins This Weekend
item.content:encoded:
Ranked play in Marathon begins very soon. In fact, it begins this Saturday, March 21, 2026. Bungie announced the news on social media.

According to the announcement, the mode is still in development for the first season. Therefore, the developers are seeking the most substantial feedback from players to help continue shaping the mode.

Marathon Ranked Play Structure

In this mode, players earn points by hitting score targets set by a special item called the Holotag. As you gain Ranked Points, you progress through six ranks. Ranks range from Bronze to Pinnacle, with each rank divided into three tiers. At the end of each season, you receive rewards based on your highest rank, encouraging players to keep playing without losing progress. Each season also offers exclusive rewards available only through Ranked play.

Rewards for the first season include gun styles and titles. Players need to reach Runner Level 25 to join Ranked matches, which are available on weekends. Each weekend features a different zone: Perimeter, Dire Marsh, or Outpost. This weekend starts with Perimeter. Crew sizes will be matched, allowing for team games or solo challenges against opposing players.

Learn more about the new mode by reading the official release at this link.

For more on Marathon from Insider Gaming, check out the latest patch notes. Insider Gaming will continue to bring the latest on Marathon as more information is released.

Have you checked out Marathon yet? What do you think of the game so far? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Geekay Announce 2026 Rainbow Six Siege Roster
item.content:encoded:
Geekay has announced its Rainbow Six Siege roster for the 2026 season as the team prepares for its Europe MENA League (EML) debut. The announcement sees former Team Empire and Virtus.pro veteran Artur ‘ShepparD’ Ipatov move to the Head Coach role.

The roster will debut on March 30 when it kicks off its Europe MENA League 2026 Kickoff campaign, with teams like Falcons, Twisted Minds, G2, and Shifters in their way.

Geekay Esports announces roster changes after Affiliate Team status secured

Geekay announce the changes as they enter the EML as an Affiliate Team, giving the organisation greater access to the circuit’s benefits and powering up their competitive potential.

Despite those benefits, Geekay lost a key piece in the offseason, losing a standout piece in Nikita ‘RORICK’ Kruchinkin to Virtus.pro.

AsK joins Geekay after a year that saw AsK go back and forth between Black Dragons and Liquid. AsK started 2025 in Black Dragons before returning to Liquid for his second stint, where he was made inactive in August before returning to Black Dragons.

The Hibana and Smoke flex finished the season with a 17th-20th position finish at the Six Invitational in Paris, after the Brazilian roster defeated AsK’s former team, Liquid, at the South America LCQ.

Italy’s Sarks departs Fnatic after the team’s mediocre placements. Despite only a 7th-place finish in EML Stage 2, Sarks has maintained a strong reputation as a mechanically sharp entry, a necessary piece in this composition.

ShepparD moves to Head Coach after a storied career, which saw the Russian/Armenian IGL become a powerhouse of the region. ShepparD and Team Empire secured their biggest title at Six Major Raleigh 2019, defeating G2 3-1 in the Grand Final.

Geekay’s 2026 Rainbow Six Siege roster is:

  • Lucas ‘Hungry’ Konstantin Reich
  • Peter ‘Pacbull’ Bull
  • Czikai ‘Rexhun999’ Máté Gábor
  • Gabriel ‘AsK’ Santos
  • Leonardo ‘Sarks’ Sarchi
  • Artur ‘ShepparD’ Ipatov (Head Coach)
  • Murarz (Assistant Coach)
  • Noor (Manager)

You can discuss how you think Geekay will fare in 2026 on Insider Gaming’s Discord server!

For more esports news, see how BLG took care of G2 at First Stand 2026.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox Insiders Can Now Disable Quick Resume
item.content:encoded:
Xbox Insiders are getting a selection of new features to test, including the ability to disable the console’s Quick Resume feature.

Microsoft is working to bring many new, helpful features to Xbox players, making their experience much more immersive. They have already announced the arrival of Gaming Copilot for the Xbox console. Now, more highly requested features are being tested by Insiders and should soon be available to all.

Xbox Insiders Get to Try More Groups on Home, Custom Colors and More

The new blog post reveals all the fresh features Xbox Insiders get to try out in the update rolling out today. Firstly, players can have more groups, with the number extended from 2 to 10. Managing these groups has also been improved, with the same reordering functionality as in games. The pinning feature has also been made more intuitive.

Next is Custom Colors, which brings a truly personalised experience to Xbox. Instead of being limited to system colors, players can use sliders to get the exact hue and shade that is unique to them. Then you have the most requested feature, the ability to disable Quick Resume on a per-game basis on Xbox consoles. This can be done by visiting Manage game & add-on > Quick Resume Settings.

Lastly, Profile Badges will highlight notable moments and milestones that players have experienced across their Xbox journey. Players will find the five most recently unlocked badges once they view their own profile or view another player’s profile in the Guide.

In other news, Numerous new additions have been announced for Xbox Game Pass, including Disco Elysium and Resident Evil 7. Additionally, Microsoft has busied itself scrubbing all traces of the This is an Xbox marketing campaign that caused blowback in 2024. What are your thoughts on Xbox testing the disabling of Quick Resume? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Fortnite teasing Italian Brainrot characters. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
